ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-24-2008 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 7/28/08 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET IN DEPT. 220 STARTING ON 7/21/08 AT 10:30AM. PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND IS DUE ON 7/16/08; DEFENDANT'S COUNTER DEMAND IS DUE BY 7/18/08. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. ADDITIONAL CONDITIONS ARE AS FOLLOW: PLAINTIFF TO COOPERATE IN ARRANGING FOR PLAINTIFF'S FATHER TO BE DEPOSED WITHIN 15 DAYS. FIRST LOOK IS SHORTENED TO 5 DAYS. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 5/5/08, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 5/27/08. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON III; CLERK: DLOK; REPORTER: GISELLE S. CASEY, CSR #8098 (206)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James J. O'Brien, Esq. and Christine Huntoon, Esq. of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Cummins Engine Company
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Baragar, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Cummins Engine Company's motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6 (negligence, strict liability and duty to warn) and taken off calendar as to issues 3, 7 and 8 (false representation and punitive damages). Plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable whether Mr. Baragar was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (engine gaskets) attributable to Cummins. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' exhibit F (Cummins documents) and H (Cummins' interrogatory responses) are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Sheldon's declaration are overruled, except the objections to 14 & 15, which are sustained. Defendant's objections to Mr. Sheldon's deposition testimony are sustained. Defendant has not sufficiently articulated what is covered by issue 6 to permit adjudication of that issue in its favor. Moreover, it appears that issue 6 seeks adjudication of less than an entire claim Plaintiffs' dismissal of its false representation and punitive damages claims render adjudication of those claims unnecessary. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-31-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Megan Cesare-Eastman, Esq. of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
John B. Goldstein, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Baragar, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. It appears that defendant satisfied its burden of production by presenting plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses on the issue of whether Mr. Baragar was exposed to any products on the USS Rogers attributable to the defendant. However, when read together and liberally construed in favor of the plaintiffs, 28:23-29:5 and 44:8-45:3 of the deposition of Mr. Pantaleoni, 95:5-13 of Mr. McLean's deposition, paragraph 4 of the declaration of Mr. Fening and the declaration of Mr. Baragar create a triable issue on this point. The objections to the cited portions of the deposition testimony of Mr. Pantaleoni and Mr. McLean and to the entirety of the declaration of Mr. Baragar are overruled. The tentative ruling does not rest on nor does the court address either the 'reentrainment' theory or argument about 'foreseeable use' of asbestos-containing products in conjunction with defendant's valves. The court rejects plaintiffs' position that the so-called 'Taylor' issue of exposure to original parts is an affirmative defense. Plaintiffs submitted a proposed order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar May-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
Jill Rowe, Esq., appeared for defendant and moving party: Kinder Morgan, Inc.
Gary L. Brayton, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and opposing party: James Baragar

RULING AFTER HEARING: Defendant Kinder Morgan's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Discovery is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion.
However, counsel are advised that as to the special and form interrogatories, the court is not likely to find merit to the objections made in the supplemental responses. Additionally, the court would not approve the responses as they reserve the right to amend the responses. The responses must be full and complete at the time they are made. Any amendments must be made as set forth in C.C.P. Section 2030.310.
The motion is moot as to the Requests For Production Of Documents as supplemental responses have been served. However, those responses are inadequate as they make objections which would not be approved, and further inadequate as they reserve the right to amend.
The motion is moot as it seeks further responses to the Requests For Admissions as supplemental responses have been served. However, those responses are inadequate as they make objections which would not be approved.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
